name: Att-Wyo Correctional Employees
description_short: Attica-Wyoming Correctional Employees FCU is a not-for-profit credit union serving correctional facility employees with savings accounts, loans, and home banking services.
description_long: Attica-Wyoming Correctional Employees Federal Credit Union (ATT-WYO CE FCU) is a member-owned financial cooperative based in Warsaw, New York. The organization serves employees of the Attica and Wyoming correctional facilities and operates as a not-for-profit institution governed by a volunteer Board of Directors elected by membership at annual meetings. Like traditional credit unions, each member holds partial ownership and benefits from the cooperative structure. The credit union has been operating long enough to establish standard supervisory and governance processes, including quarterly statement preparation and regular supervisory committee member verification procedures. ATT-WYO CE FCU offers a range of consumer financial products including savings accounts, vehicle loans (new and used cars, trucks, and recreational vehicles), home equity loans, and home improvement financing. Members access accounts through real-time online home banking with login and enrollment capabilities. The credit union advertises competitive rates, with recent offerings at 4.74% for vehicles, 6.49% for home equity, and 6.74% for recreational loans. Savings deposits are federally insured by the NCUA up to $250,000 per account. What distinguishes ATT-WYO CE FCU is its niche market focus on correctional facility employees, creating a specialized member community with shared professional backgrounds. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, the organization returns earnings directly to members through higher dividend rates, lower loan rates, and expanded service offerings rather than distributing profits to shareholders. The organization actively recruits volunteers for committee positions and maintains a local, community-focused approach with direct member contact for account verification and customer service. The company appears well-established and legitimate, with appropriate legal disclosures regarding third-party links and privacy policies. However, as a small regional credit union, it likely has limited branch locations and may have fewer digital conveniences compared to larger national banks or credit unions, and membership is restricted to employees of the specified correctional facilities. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings accounts with NCUA insurance up to $250,000
Real-time online home banking with account access
Direct member account verification and dispute resolution
Annual membership meetings with Board of Directors elections
pros: Member deposits federally insured by NCUA up to $250,000
Competitive loan rates: 4.74% for vehicles, 6.49% home equity, 6.74% recreational loans
Real-time online home banking access available 24/7
Not-for-profit structure returns earnings to members as higher dividends and lower rates
Volunteer-governed board elected directly by membership
Earnings reinvested in expanded member services rather than shareholder profits
Active member engagement with committee opportunities
cons: Membership restricted to Attica and Wyoming correctional facility employees only
Limited geographic reach as a small regional credit union with single headquarters in Warsaw, NY
Website login functionality appears to have technical issues (error message: 'Your account was not found')
No information provided about mobile app availability or advanced digital banking features
Small institution may have limited branch locations and reduced service hours compared to national banks
best_for: Employees of Attica and Wyoming correctional facilities seeking member-owned financial services
Correctional workers wanting to finance vehicle purchases or home improvements at competitive rates
Individuals prioritizing NCUA insurance protection and cooperative ownership structure over large institution services
diagnosis: ATT-WYO CE FCU is best for correctional facility employees at Attica or Wyoming facilities seeking a member-owned credit union with competitive vehicle and home loan rates. The main caveat is that membership is restricted to employees of these two specific facilities, making it inaccessible to the general public, and as a small regional institution it may lack the digital convenience and branch accessibility of larger national credit unions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
